project.name: hermes-agent
project.description: Hermes Agent is Nous Research's self-improving AI agent: a TUI plus messaging gateway (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, CLI) with an autonomous learning loop that creates and refines skills, curates memory, and runs against any LLM provider. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Classifies a tool result as success, failure, timeout, or other category for guardrail accounting.

nodes.name: ChatCompletionsTransport

nodes.name: ToolCall

nodes.name: NormalizedResponse

nodes.name: usage_pricing.py
nodes.summary: Centralized usage normalization and cost estimation engine that resolves billing routes, looks up per-provider pricing, and computes token-level cost across all supported models.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Decimal arithmetic for monetary precision and consolidates pricing from official docs, OpenRouter, and model metadata into a single canonical interface.

nodes.name: CanonicalUsage
nodes.summary: Canonical usage dataclass aggregating input, output, cached, and reasoning token counts across providers.

nodes.name: PricingEntry
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding per-token pricing rates for a model along with currency and source attribution.

nodes.name: resolve_billing_route
nodes.summary: Determines the effective billing route (provider + canonical model name) used to look up pricing for a given provider/model pair.

nodes.name: _pricing_entry_from_metadata
nodes.summary: Constructs a PricingEntry from model metadata, falling back across official docs and OpenRouter sources.

nodes.name: get_pricing_entry
nodes.summary: Public lookup returning the best available PricingEntry for a provider/model pair, consulting multiple data sources in priority order.

nodes.name: normalize_usage
nodes.summary: Converts heterogeneous provider-specific usage payloads into a CanonicalUsage record with consistent token categories.

nodes.name: estimate_usage_cost
nodes.summary: Computes a CostResult by combining canonical usage with the resolved pricing entry, applying per-token rates with Decimal precision.

nodes.name: has_known_pricing
nodes.summary: Returns whether pricing data is available for a given provider/model so callers can short-circuit cost reporting.

nodes.name: format_token_count_compact

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the cron subsystem; re-exports the job store and scheduler so callers can import from `cron` directly.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using re-exports.

nodes.name: jobs.py
nodes.summary: Job store and scheduling primitives for hermes-agent's cron system: parses schedule expressions, persists jobs to disk with secure permissions, and computes next-run times for recurring and one-shot jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of timezone-aware datetimes and atomic file I/O for crash-safe job persistence.

nodes.name: scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Cron tick loop and job runner that builds prompts, invokes the agent runtime, scans for prompt-injection, and routes results to delivery platforms (gateway adapters, home targets, plugin env-driven channels).
nodes.languageNotes: Coordinates locking, multi-platform delivery routing, and prompt scanning across many subsystems.

nodes.name: CronPromptInjectionBlocked
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the assembled cron prompt fails the prompt-injection scan and the run must be aborted.

nodes.name: parse_duration
nodes.summary: Parses a human-readable duration string (e.g. '5m', '2h') into a timedelta.

nodes.name: parse_schedule
nodes.summary: Parses a schedule expression (cron, interval, one-shot ISO timestamp, or natural relative) into a normalized job-schedule descriptor.

nodes.name: _compute_grace_seconds
nodes.summary: Computes the grace window for a missed cron run based on schedule type and configured tolerance.

nodes.name: compute_next_run
nodes.summary: Computes the next scheduled run time for a job from its schedule descriptor and reference time.

nodes.name: load_jobs
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted job list from disk, normalizing each record on read.

nodes.name: save_jobs
nodes.summary: Persists the job list to disk atomically with secure file permissions.

nodes.name: create_job
nodes.summary: Validates inputs and creates a new cron job record (parsing schedule, normalizing skills/workdir, computing first run) then persists it.

nodes.name: update_job
nodes.summary: Applies a partial update to an existing job record and persists the new state.

nodes.name: pause_job
nodes.summary: Marks a job as paused so it is skipped on subsequent ticks.

nodes.name: resume_job
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused job, recomputing its next-run time.

nodes.name: trigger_job
nodes.summary: Forces a job to be due immediately on the next tick.

nodes.name: remove_job
nodes.summary: Deletes a job from the persisted store by id.

nodes.name: mark_job_run
nodes.summary: Records the outcome of a job execution (last run time, status, error info, run history) and persists the update.

nodes.name: advance_next_run
nodes.summary: Advances a job's next-run time after execution, handling one-shot completion.

nodes.name: _get_due_jobs_locked
nodes.summary: Selects all jobs due to run at the current tick, applying grace windows, locking, and concurrency rules.

nodes.name: save_job_output
nodes.summary: Writes a job's run output to disk under the job's output directory with secure permissions.

nodes.name: rewrite_skill_refs
nodes.summary: Rewrites skill references in job prompts to canonical forms after skill renames or relocations.

nodes.name: _resolve_cron_enabled_toolsets
nodes.summary: Resolves which tool sets a cron job is permitted to use based on configuration and plugin env vars.

nodes.name: _resolve_single_delivery_target
nodes.summary: Resolves a single delivery target descriptor (platform, chat_id, thread) from configured deliver tokens and home/env vars.

nodes.name: _resolve_delivery_targets
nodes.summary: Expands and resolves the full list of delivery targets for a job's output.

nodes.name: _send_media_via_adapter
nodes.summary: Sends media artifacts (images, files) produced by a job through the appropriate gateway platform adapter.

nodes.name: _deliver_result
nodes.summary: Delivers a job's textual and media results to all resolved targets, handling per-platform formatting, redaction, and error reporting.

nodes.name: _get_script_timeout
nodes.summary: Determines the execution timeout for a job's script based on configuration and overrides.

nodes.name: _run_job_script
nodes.summary: Executes a job's shell-script body in its workdir with timeout, capturing stdout/stderr and exit status.

nodes.name: _build_job_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the agent prompt for a cron job including system context, skills metadata, time, and user instructions.

nodes.name: _scan_assembled_cron_prompt
nodes.summary: Scans the assembled cron prompt for prompt-injection patterns and raises CronPromptInjectionBlocked when detected.

nodes.name: run_job
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs a single cron job: builds prompt or runs script, invokes the agent runtime, captures output, performs delivery, and records run history.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Cron tick loop that polls due jobs, acquires per-job locks, dispatches `run_job`, and updates job state.

nodes.name: SOUL.md

nodes.name: entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Docker/Podman entrypoint script that drops root via gosu, fixes volume ownership, bootstraps config files (.env, config.yaml, SOUL.md, auth.json) into the mounted data volume, optionally launches the dashboard, then execs the hermes CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gosu for privilege drop, supports both Docker and rootless Podman, and dispatches between direct exec vs hermes-wrapped invocation based on whether the first arg resolves on PATH.

nodes.languageNotes: Implements an OpenAI Chat Completions surface with response store and request fingerprinting for idempotency.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines BasePlatformAdapter and shared message-event primitives (MessageEvent, SendResult, EphemeralReply) plus extensive utilities for proxying, media caching, and channel resolution shared across all platform adapters.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the abstract substrate for every platform adapter; encodes proxy detection, media caching, and channel/skill resolution logic.

nodes.name: bluebubbles.py
nodes.summary: BlueBubbles iMessage adapter that bridges the Hermes agent to macOS iMessage via the BlueBubbles relay server's HTTP and websocket APIs.

nodes.name: dingtalk.py
nodes.summary: DingTalk enterprise messenger adapter handling webhook signing, callback ingestion, and outbound rich-message delivery for the Alibaba DingTalk platform.

nodes.name: discord.py
nodes.summary: Discord bot adapter providing text and voice channel integration, including a custom VoiceReceiver for live audio capture, slash commands, and DM auth gating.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest single adapter: integrates discord.py, voice receive/playback, and component callbacks for approval flows.

nodes.name: email.py
nodes.summary: Email adapter that polls IMAP for inbound messages, decodes MIME parts and attachments, and sends agent replies via SMTP, treating email threads as agent sessions.

nodes.name: feishu.py
nodes.summary: Feishu/Lark adapter handling event subscriptions, post/card rendering, mention parsing, batch state, and group-level rules for the largest enterprise integration in the codebase.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes bespoke Feishu post-payload parser/renderer and rich content normalization across forward/share/interactive message types.

nodes.name: feishu_comment.py
nodes.summary: Specialised handler for Feishu Drive document comment events: builds prompts from comment threads and runs an isolated agent session per comment to generate replies.

nodes.name: feishu_comment_rules.py
nodes.summary: Configures and resolves authorization and routing rules for Feishu document-comment automation, including pairing management and CLI status checks.

nodes.name: helpers.py
nodes.summary: Cross-platform helper utilities for adapters: message deduplication, text-batch aggregation, thread-participation tracking, markdown stripping, and phone redaction.

nodes.name: homeassistant.py
nodes.summary: Home Assistant adapter that integrates the agent with Home Assistant conversation/notify services for smart-home interaction.

nodes.name: matrix.py
nodes.summary: Matrix protocol adapter with E2EE support via olm crypto, handling room sync, encrypted message dispatch, and approval prompts within the Matrix federation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses optional olm/megolm crypto state store to support end-to-end encrypted rooms.

nodes.name: build_channel_directory
nodes.summary: Aggregates channel entries from configured Discord/Slack platforms and existing sessions into a unified directory structure.

nodes.name: resolve_channel_name
nodes.summary: Resolves a free-form channel query against the directory, returning the best-matching channel id and platform.

nodes.name: format_directory_for_display
nodes.summary: Renders the channel directory as human-readable text grouped by platform for CLI/agent display.

nodes.name: GatewayConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level dataclass aggregating all platform, home-channel, streaming, and global gateway settings consumed across the messaging stack.

nodes.name: PlatformConfig
nodes.summary: Per-platform configuration dataclass storing credentials, behaviour toggles, and feature flags for an individual messaging integration.

nodes.name: Platform
nodes.summary: Enum-like descriptor for supported messaging platforms, exposing canonical names, defaults, and capabilities.

nodes.name: StreamingConfig
nodes.summary: Streaming-related configuration (chunk sizes, buffering, flush intervals) used by adapters that push token-by-token agent output.

nodes.name: HomeChannel
nodes.summary: Defines the user's home channel — the default platform/channel pairing the agent uses when no explicit destination is provided.

nodes.name: SessionResetPolicy
nodes.summary: Policy controlling when conversational sessions are reset for a given channel (idle, manual, never).

nodes.name: load_gateway_config
nodes.summary: Loads and assembles the full GatewayConfig from disk and environment, merging defaults, file settings, and overrides into a validated config tree.

nodes.name: _apply_env_overrides
nodes.summary: Applies environment variable overrides onto a GatewayConfig instance, mapping hundreds of HERMES_* envvars to their corresponding config fields.

nodes.name: _validate_gateway_config
nodes.summary: Validates the assembled GatewayConfig, raising structured errors for missing credentials and incompatible option combinations.

nodes.name: DeliveryRouter
nodes.summary: Routes outbound messages to the correct platform adapter based on a DeliveryTarget, handling fallbacks and channel resolution.

nodes.name: DeliveryTarget
nodes.summary: Descriptor identifying where a message should be delivered (platform, channel, thread) used by the DeliveryRouter.

nodes.name: resolve_display_setting
nodes.summary: Resolves a per-platform display setting by walking platform overrides, defaults, and global flags to a normalized value.

nodes.name: HookRegistry
nodes.summary: Registry that tracks named hook callbacks and dispatches lifecycle events (pre/post-message, errors) to subscribed extensions.

nodes.name: mirror_to_session
nodes.summary: Mirrors a platform message into the appropriate Hermes session store, choosing JSONL or SQLite backends based on configuration.

nodes.name: _find_session_id
nodes.summary: Locates the session id associated with an incoming platform event for mirroring purposes.

nodes.name: PairingStore
nodes.summary: Disk-backed store managing user-channel pairings with atomic secure writes; gates which platform identities are authorized to invoke the agent.

nodes.name: PlatformRegistry
nodes.summary: Registry that records platform adapter classes and metadata, supporting both built-in and plugin-contributed integrations.

nodes.name: PlatformEntry
nodes.summary: Metadata entry describing a registered platform: adapter class, display name, capabilities, and configuration shape.

nodes.name: platform_httpx_limits
nodes.summary: Builds httpx.Limits for platform HTTP clients with sensible connection and keepalive caps tuned for messaging integrations.

nodes.name: APIServerAdapter
nodes.summary: FastAPI-style platform adapter exposing OpenAI-compatible chat completions, embeddings, and response endpoints backed by the Hermes agent.

nodes.name: ResponseStore
nodes.summary: In-memory store of OpenAI Responses API objects with retrieval, streaming completion, and cancellation support.

nodes.name: _IdempotencyCache
nodes.summary: TTL-bound cache keyed by request fingerprint that suppresses duplicate API submissions.

nodes.name: _normalize_multimodal_content
nodes.summary: Normalizes incoming OpenAI multimodal message content (text, images, audio) into the canonical form expected by the agent runtime.

nodes.name: BasePlatformAdapter
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for every messaging platform adapter, providing event lifecycle, agent invocation, media caching, approval flow, and config resolution helpers.

nodes.name: MessageEvent
nodes.summary: Canonical incoming message event used across all adapters, carrying sender, channel, content, attachments, and platform-specific metadata.

nodes.name: EphemeralReply
nodes.summary: Reply primitive representing an ephemeral, often non-persistent message back to the user (e.g., status indicators).

nodes.name: MessageType
nodes.summary: Enumeration of message kinds (text, image, audio, document, etc.) recognised by the gateway.

nodes.name: ProcessingOutcome
nodes.summary: Enumeration describing the result of processing an incoming message event (handled, skipped, failed).

nodes.name: SendResult
nodes.summary: Return type for adapter send operations carrying success status and provider-specific message identifiers.

nodes.name: resolve_proxy_url
nodes.summary: Resolves the appropriate HTTP/HTTPS proxy URL for a given target host, honouring env vars and macOS system proxy detection.

nodes.name: cache_image_from_url
nodes.summary: Downloads a remote image to the platform-shared cache directory with SSRF guards and content-type validation.

nodes.name: resolve_channel_skills
nodes.summary: Resolves the set of skills enabled for a given channel by combining global, platform, and channel-scoped overrides.

nodes.name: BlueBubblesAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter that connects to a BlueBubbles relay server to send and receive iMessage traffic on behalf of the Hermes agent.

nodes.name: DingTalkAdapter
nodes.summary: DingTalk adapter that ingests signed callback events and sends rich text/markdown/card replies into DingTalk groups and DMs.

nodes.name: _IncomingHandler
nodes.summary: HTTP handler routing inbound DingTalk webhook requests into the adapter's processing pipeline.

nodes.name: DiscordAdapter
nodes.summary: Discord platform adapter built on discord.py, supporting text channels, DMs, slash commands, voice mode, and component-based approval prompts.

nodes.name: VoiceReceiver
nodes.summary: Custom Discord voice receiver capturing live PCM audio per speaker and feeding it into the agent's transcription pipeline.

nodes.name: EmailAdapter
nodes.summary: IMAP/SMTP-based email adapter that turns email threads into agent sessions, decoding attachments and detecting automated senders.

nodes.name: _extract_attachments
nodes.summary: Walks a MIME tree to extract user-visible attachments and inline media for forwarding to the agent.

nodes.name: FeishuAdapter
nodes.summary: Feishu/Lark adapter handling event subscriptions, post and card rendering, mentions, and group rules for full-featured Feishu integration.

nodes.name: yuanbao_sticker.py
nodes.summary: Sticker library and fuzzy-match search utilities for the Yuanbao platform, including face/sticker message body builders.

nodes.name: get_sticker_by_name
nodes.summary: Resolves a sticker entry by exact or normalized name from the Yuanbao sticker catalog.

nodes.name: _multiset_char_hit_ratio
nodes.summary: Computes character-multiset overlap ratio used as one signal for sticker fuzzy matching.

nodes.name: _longest_subsequence_ratio
nodes.summary: Computes ratio based on longest common subsequence length for sticker name similarity scoring.

nodes.name: _score_field
nodes.summary: Composite scorer combining multiple similarity signals for ranking sticker matches against a query.

nodes.name: search_stickers
nodes.summary: Returns ranked sticker matches for a free-text query using composite similarity scoring.

nodes.name: build_face_msg_body
nodes.summary: Constructs the Yuanbao API message body payload for sending a face/emoji asset.

nodes.name: build_sticker_msg_body
nodes.summary: Constructs the Yuanbao API message body payload for sending a sticker asset.

nodes.name: restart.py
nodes.summary: Helper for parsing the gateway restart drain timeout from configuration.

nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: Massive gateway runtime entrypoint orchestrating platform adapters, session lifecycle, agent dispatch, cron ticking, and process management for the Hermes messaging gateway.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 16k+ line module containing the GatewayRunner god-class plus dozens of helpers; a candidate for decomposition.

nodes.name: _telegramize_command_mentions
nodes.summary: Rewrites command mentions to Telegram-compatible @bot suffix syntax for outbound messages.

nodes.name: _coerce_gateway_timestamp
nodes.summary: Coerces heterogeneous timestamp inputs (epoch, ISO, datetime) into a normalized form for gateway events.

nodes.name: _is_fresh_gateway_interruption
nodes.summary: Determines whether a gateway interruption signal is recent enough to warrant auto-resume handling.

nodes.name: _build_replay_entry
nodes.summary: Builds a structured replay entry from a transcript record for session restoration.

nodes.name: _last_transcript_timestamp
nodes.summary: Reads the most recent timestamp from a transcript file to identify the freshest message in a session.

nodes.name: _ensure_ssl_certs
nodes.summary: Ensures CA bundle and SSL certificate environment variables are populated for outbound HTTPS calls.

nodes.name: _reload_runtime_env_preserving_config_authority
nodes.summary: Reloads runtime environment variables while keeping the existing config file as authoritative.

nodes.name: _resolve_runtime_agent_kwargs
nodes.summary: Builds the keyword-argument bundle for invoking the runtime agent given current gateway settings.

nodes.name: _try_resolve_fallback_provider
nodes.summary: Attempts to select a fallback model provider when the primary provider is unavailable.

nodes.name: _build_media_placeholder
nodes.summary: Generates a textual placeholder representing media (image/audio/file) attachments in transcripts.

nodes.name: _skill_slug_from_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses YAML frontmatter from a skill file to extract its slug identifier.

nodes.name: _check_unavailable_skill
nodes.summary: Validates whether a referenced skill is missing or disabled and produces a user-facing message.

nodes.name: _load_gateway_config
nodes.summary: Loads the gateway-level configuration block, applying defaults when absent.

nodes.name: _resolve_hermes_bin
nodes.summary: Resolves the path to the hermes CLI binary used to spawn child agent processes.

nodes.name: _parse_session_key
nodes.summary: Decomposes a session key string into its platform/chat/user components.

nodes.name: _format_gateway_process_notification
nodes.summary: Formats a human-readable notification message describing a gateway process state change.

nodes.name: _normalize_empty_agent_response
nodes.summary: Replaces empty agent responses with a fallback message so users always receive a reply.

nodes.name: _should_clear_resume_pending_after_turn
nodes.summary: Decides whether the resume-pending marker should be cleared after a completed agent turn.

nodes.name: _start_cron_ticker
nodes.summary: Starts the background cron ticker thread that fires scheduled tasks while the gateway is alive.

nodes.name: start_gateway
nodes.summary: Top-level entrypoint that bootstraps the GatewayRunner, registers platform adapters, and runs the gateway lifecycle loop.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main wrapper invoking start_gateway with command-line arguments.

nodes.name: GatewayRunner
nodes.summary: Core gateway orchestrator coordinating platform adapters, session storage, agent invocation, queueing, status, and recovery for all messaging channels.
nodes.languageNotes: Spans ~14400 lines with dozens of methods - effectively the gateway's core service object.

nodes.name: runtime_footer.py
nodes.summary: Builds the runtime status footer line displayed in terminal/messaging output (cwd, model, version).

nodes.name: resolve_footer_config
nodes.summary: Resolves user-configurable footer template and visibility settings.

nodes.name: format_runtime_footer
nodes.summary: Formats the runtime footer string by interpolating runtime metadata into the configured template.

nodes.name: build_footer_line
nodes.summary: Composes the final footer line including separator and styling for terminal rendering.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Persistent session store for the gateway - tracks SessionEntry/SessionContext records keyed by platform/chat/user, including hashing, persistence, and expiry policies.

nodes.name: _hash_chat_id
nodes.summary: Generates a stable, privacy-preserving hash for a chat identifier.

nodes.name: _discord_tools_loaded
nodes.summary: Detects whether discord-specific tooling has been loaded for the active session context.

nodes.name: build_session_context_prompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the rich session-context system prompt fragment describing platform, participants, and capabilities for the agent.

nodes.name: is_shared_multi_user_session
nodes.summary: Determines whether a session is a shared group/channel context with multiple users.

nodes.name: build_session_key
nodes.summary: Builds a canonical session key string from platform, chat, and user identifiers with appropriate hashing.

nodes.name: build_session_context
nodes.summary: Factory that assembles a SessionContext object from raw platform message fields.

nodes.name: SessionSource
nodes.summary: Value object describing the source platform/channel of a session, with serialization helpers.

nodes.name: SessionContext
nodes.summary: Per-message session context bundle describing chat, sender, and platform metadata used by the agent.

nodes.name: SessionEntry
nodes.summary: Persistent session record with metadata, history pointers, and (de)serialization support.

nodes.name: SessionStore
nodes.summary: On-disk session repository with locking, lazy loading, expiry, and CRUD operations over SessionEntry records.

nodes.name: session_context.py
nodes.summary: Sets and clears per-session environment variables consumed by spawned agent subprocesses.

nodes.name: set_session_vars
nodes.summary: Exports session metadata as environment variables for the current process and child agents.

nodes.name: clear_session_vars
nodes.summary: Removes previously set session environment variables to reset state between turns.

nodes.name: get_session_env
nodes.summary: Returns the current session environment dictionary suitable for passing to subprocess invocations.

nodes.name: status.py
nodes.summary: Process and runtime-status management for the gateway: PID files, runtime locks, scoped locks, takeover/planned-stop markers, and process liveness checks.

nodes.name: terminate_pid
nodes.summary: Sends termination signals to a PID with graceful-then-forceful escalation.

nodes.name: _looks_like_gateway_process
nodes.summary: Heuristically determines whether a given PID's cmdline appears to be a hermes gateway process.

nodes.name: _read_pid_record
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the gateway PID record JSON file with error tolerance.

nodes.name: _cleanup_invalid_pid_path
nodes.summary: Removes stale or corrupt PID files belonging to dead processes.

nodes.name: _pid_exists
nodes.summary: Robust PID existence check across platforms, validating cmdline and start time when possible.

nodes.name: acquire_gateway_runtime_lock
nodes.summary: Acquires the exclusive gateway runtime lock to ensure only one gateway instance runs.

nodes.name: release_gateway_runtime_lock
nodes.summary: Releases the gateway runtime lock acquired via acquire_gateway_runtime_lock.

nodes.name: is_gateway_runtime_lock_active
nodes.summary: Checks whether the gateway runtime lock is currently held by a live process.

nodes.name: write_pid_file
nodes.summary: Writes the gateway PID record to disk including pid, start time, and cmdline metadata.

nodes.name: write_runtime_status
nodes.summary: Persists a runtime status snapshot capturing the gateway's current operational state.

nodes.name: remove_pid_file
nodes.summary: Removes the gateway PID file during shutdown after verifying ownership.

nodes.name: acquire_scoped_lock
nodes.summary: Acquires a named scoped lock (e.g., per-session) using a file-based mutex with stale-lock detection.

nodes.name: plugins.py
nodes.summary: Core plugin manager subsystem that loads, validates, and exposes plugin manifests, contexts, and toolsets to the rest of the CLI.

nodes.name: plugins_cmd.py
nodes.summary: CLI subcommand implementation for plugin install/update/remove/enable/disable/list/toggle workflows including dashboard hooks and an interactive composite UI.

nodes.name: profile_distribution.py
nodes.summary: Distribution packaging and installation logic that fetches, validates, and installs hermes profile bundles with semver-based dependency checks.

nodes.name: profiles.py
nodes.summary: Hermes profile lifecycle management covering create/delete/rename/import/export, alias/wrapper scripts, and shell completion generation.

nodes.name: providers.py
nodes.summary: Provider catalogue and resolution helpers that map provider definitions to API modes, overlays, and runtime settings.

nodes.name: pt_input_extras.py
nodes.summary: Installs Shift+Enter and Ctrl+Enter key bindings for prompt_toolkit input to allow multiline submission.

nodes.name: pty_bridge.py
nodes.summary: PTY bridge wrapping a subprocess in a pseudo-terminal so the CLI can mirror its stdio over the dashboard websocket.

nodes.name: relaunch.py
nodes.summary: Helpers that resolve the hermes binary, build inherited CLI flags, and re-execute the process under a new interpreter or environment.

nodes.name: runtime_provider.py
nodes.summary: Runtime provider resolution engine that picks the active credential pool entry and produces a configured runtime for the chosen model.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Interactive setup wizard guiding users through provider, gateway, tools, TTS, terminal backend and migration configuration.

nodes.name: skills_config.py
nodes.summary: Skills configuration command that lets users toggle skill categories per messaging platform.

nodes.name: skills_hub.py
nodes.summary: Skills hub CLI implementation: search, browse, install, audit, publish, snapshot import/export of skills and a slash-command handler.

nodes.name: skin_engine.py
nodes.summary: Skin/theme engine that loads YAML-defined visual themes and emits prompt-toolkit style overrides for the CLI.

nodes.name: slack_cli.py
nodes.summary: Slack manifest CLI that builds and prints a complete Slack app manifest for installation.

nodes.name: status.py
nodes.summary: Status command that aggregates provider, gateway, model, and credential health into a formatted CLI report.

nodes.name: stdio.py

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Langfuse observability plugin covering enable/disable workflow, required credentials, verification steps, and optional tuning environment variables.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Langfuse observability plugin that hooks into LLM and tool call lifecycle events to emit traces, spans, generations, usage and cost metrics to a Langfuse backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of module-level state (_TRACE_STATE) keyed by task/session, careful payload normalization (e.g. read_file payload parsing), and Decimal-based cost computation.

nodes.name: TraceState
nodes.summary: Dataclass-like container holding the active Langfuse root trace context, span, and per-task observation registries (generations, tools, turn tool calls).

nodes.name: _get_langfuse
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a Langfuse client from environment-supplied credentials, gracefully no-oping if the SDK or required keys are missing.

nodes.name: _normalize_read_file_payload
nodes.summary: Specializes payload normalization for read_file tool outputs by parsing line-numbered content into a structured preview suitable for Langfuse display.

nodes.name: _safe_value
nodes.summary: Recursively converts arbitrary Python values into JSON-safe, length-bounded representations for trace serialization, with optional embedded JSON-string parsing.

nodes.name: _serialize_messages
nodes.summary: Serializes a list of chat messages (role, content, tool_call_id, name) into a sanitized form for tracing.

nodes.name: _serialize_tool_calls
nodes.summary: Serializes assistant tool_call objects into a sanitized list with parsed JSON arguments where possible.

nodes.name: _usage_and_cost
nodes.summary: Normalizes provider usage data and computes per-token costs (input/output/cached/reasoning) using the agent pricing table, returning structured usage_details and cost_details.

nodes.name: _start_root_trace
nodes.summary: Creates a new Langfuse root trace and root span for a task/session, propagating attributes (session_id, user_id, tags) and seeding the per-task TraceState.

nodes.name: _start_child_observation
nodes.summary: Starts a child observation (generation or tool span) under the active root span, attaching input, metadata, model, and parameters.

nodes.name: _end_observation
nodes.summary: Finalizes a Langfuse observation by writing output, metadata, usage and cost details and ending the span.

nodes.name: _finish_trace
nodes.summary: Closes any pending generation/tool observations, sets final trace IO, ends the root span, flushes the Langfuse client, and removes the TraceState entry.

nodes.name: on_pre_llm_call
nodes.summary: Hook fired before an LLM turn starts; ensures a root trace exists for the task/session and seeds turn-level metadata.

nodes.name: on_pre_llm_request
nodes.summary: Hook fired before each LLM HTTP request; rotates any in-flight generation observation and starts a fresh generation span with serialized message context.

nodes.name: on_post_llm_call
nodes.summary: Hook fired after an LLM response; serializes the assistant message and tool calls, computes usage/cost from response metadata, ends the generation observation, and finishes the trace when no tool calls remain.

nodes.name: on_pre_tool_call
nodes.summary: Hook fired before a tool invocation; opens a tool span on the active trace with normalized arguments.

nodes.name: on_post_tool_call
nodes.summary: Hook fired after a tool returns; closes the matching tool span with normalized output and updates trace state.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Plugin registration entrypoint that subscribes the Langfuse hook functions for pre/post LLM call, LLM request, and tool call lifecycle events.

nodes.name: plugin.yaml
nodes.summary: Plugin manifest for the Langfuse observability plugin declaring required environment variables (Langfuse keys/host) and the lifecycle hooks it registers.

nodes.name: parseltongue.py
nodes.summary: Text obfuscation toolkit applying many transforms (leetspeak, unicode, bubble, morse, base64, fullwidth, layered) to evade keyword-based content filters during red-teaming.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements 30+ small transform functions composed via dispatch; favors functional/atomic helpers.

nodes.name: _apply_unicode
nodes.summary: Maps ASCII letters to visually similar Unicode codepoints to bypass token-level filters.

nodes.name: _apply_bubble
nodes.summary: Converts text to circled (bubble) Unicode characters.

nodes.name: _apply_fullwidth
nodes.summary: Converts ASCII to fullwidth Unicode equivalents.

nodes.name: _apply_piglatin
nodes.summary: Transforms input text into Pig Latin to defeat exact-string filters.

nodes.name: _apply_mathbold
nodes.summary: Replaces ASCII letters with Mathematical Bold Unicode codepoints.

nodes.name: _apply_mathitalic
nodes.summary: Replaces ASCII letters with Mathematical Italic Unicode codepoints.

nodes.name: _apply_leetunicode
nodes.summary: Combines leetspeak substitution with Unicode confusables for layered obfuscation.

nodes.name: _apply_bubblespaced
nodes.summary: Bubble Unicode plus inter-character spacing for compound evasion.

nodes.name: _apply_acrostic
nodes.summary: Hides target message as acrostic first letters of generated lines.

nodes.name: _apply_dottedunicode
nodes.summary: Inserts combining dot marks between Unicode-substituted characters.

nodes.name: _apply_fullwidthmixed
nodes.summary: Mixes fullwidth and regular ASCII to break tokenization.

nodes.name: _apply_triplelayer
nodes.summary: Composes three obfuscation transforms in sequence for maximum evasion.

nodes.name: to_bubble
nodes.summary: Public helper that converts text to bubble Unicode form.

nodes.name: to_morse
nodes.summary: Converts text to Morse code representation.

nodes.name: detect_triggers
nodes.summary: Scans input for known trigger keywords likely to be filtered, returning matches for targeted obfuscation.

nodes.name: obfuscate_query
nodes.summary: High-level entry point that selects and applies an obfuscation strategy to a user query for filter evasion.

nodes.name: generate_variants
nodes.summary: Generates multiple obfuscated variants of a query across different transform strategies.

nodes.name: escalate_encoding
nodes.summary: Iteratively escalates obfuscation intensity by composing additional transforms on each retry.

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: entry.py

nodes.name: _install_sidecar_publisher
nodes.summary: Installs a TeeTransport and WsPublisherTransport on process startup so all output is mirrored to a WebSocket sidecar publisher.

nodes.name: _log_signal
nodes.summary: Signal handler that logs received OS signals with stack traces of all threads to a file, then initiates a graceful shutdown with a configurable grace period before hard exiting.

nodes.name: _log_exit
nodes.summary: Writes an exit reason and timestamp to a log file on process termination, providing diagnostics for abnormal shutdowns.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main entry function that reads a JSON-RPC request from stdin, validates it, dispatches it to the server, and writes the response to stdout before exiting.

nodes.name: event_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Provides a WebSocket-backed transport that asynchronously publishes serialized events from a background worker thread to a connected WebSocket endpoint.

nodes.name: WsPublisherTransport
nodes.summary: Thread-safe transport that queues JSON-serialized events and drains them to a WebSocket connection via a dedicated background thread.

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: Provides text rendering helpers for the TUI gateway, including message and diff formatters and a streaming renderer factory used by the server layer.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: Core TUI gateway server implementing the full JSON-RPC dispatch loop over stdio/WebSocket, managing agent sessions, tool callbacks, model switching, history compression, voice mode, browser integration, and all TUI interaction methods (6555 lines, highly complex).
nodes.languageNotes: The many anonymous `_` functions are method handlers registered via a decorator pattern (`@method('name')`), making the file essentially a large RPC server with 170+ registered methods.

nodes.name: _SlashWorker
nodes.summary: Manages a subprocess running the slash command worker, draining its stdout/stderr in dedicated threads and routing output back through the session.

nodes.name: dispatch
nodes.summary: Core JSON-RPC dispatch function that routes incoming requests to registered method handlers and writes responses to the transport.

nodes.name: _run_prompt_submit
nodes.summary: Handles the main prompt submission flow: enriches user input with images, runs the agent, streams responses, emits tool events, and manages session state (359 lines, most complex handler).

nodes.name: _make_agent
nodes.summary: Constructs and configures a new Hermes agent instance with the resolved model, toolsets, personality prompt, and session callbacks.

nodes.name: _init_session
nodes.summary: Initializes a new TUI session by restoring history, setting terminal columns, and registering the session in the active sessions map.

nodes.name: _compress_session_history
nodes.summary: Compresses the conversation history of an active session by summarizing past messages to reduce context window usage.

nodes.name: _start_agent_build
nodes.summary: Builds the agent for a session asynchronously, resolving model, toolsets, and runtime configuration before making the agent ready for use.

nodes.name: _load_enabled_toolsets
nodes.summary: Reads and resolves the set of enabled toolsets for the current session from configuration, supporting tool whitelisting and blacklisting (125 lines).

nodes.name: _browser_connect
nodes.summary: Handles browser CDP connection requests by probing the given URL, normalizing it, and registering a live browser session for use by browser tools.

nodes.name: _apply_model_switch
nodes.summary: Applies a model switch request to the current session, persisting the new model choice and restarting the agent with the updated configuration.

nodes.name: _wire_callbacks
nodes.summary: Registers tool start, complete, and progress callbacks on the agent for a given session so tool events are forwarded to the TUI.

nodes.name: slash_worker.py
nodes.summary: Subprocess entry point for the slash command worker that reads commands from stdin and delegates them to the CLI handler, sending responses back over stdout.

nodes.name: _run
nodes.summary: Reads a single slash command invocation from stdin, dispatches it through the CLI handler, and writes the JSON result to stdout.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point loop for the slash command subprocess that reads commands from stdin and processes them via _run until the connection is closed.

nodes.name: transport.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract Transport interface and concrete StdioTransport and TeeTransport implementations, plus context-variable-based transport binding used throughout the gateway.

nodes.name: Transport
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the write/close interface for all gateway transport implementations.

nodes.name: StdioTransport
nodes.summary: Transport implementation that writes JSON-encoded messages to stdout with length-prefixed framing, used as the primary channel between the TUI and the gateway process.

nodes.name: TeeTransport
nodes.summary: Transport multiplexer that forwards writes to two underlying transports simultaneously, enabling simultaneous stdio and WebSocket delivery.

nodes.name: ws.py
nodes.summary: Implements the WebSocket transport layer for the TUI gateway, providing WSTransport for async message delivery and handle_ws for the WebSocket connection loop.

nodes.name: WSTransport
nodes.summary: WebSocket-backed transport that supports both synchronous and async message writes with error recovery via safe send wrapper.

nodes.name: handle_ws
nodes.summary: WebSocket connection handler that binds a WSTransport on connect, replays buffered events to new clients, and forwards incoming messages to the server dispatch loop.

nodes.summary: Renders the floating overlay layer including the prompt zone (approval, clarify, sudo, secret prompts) and all modal overlays (model picker, session picker, skills hub, agents overlay, FPS overlay).

nodes.name: FloatingOverlays
nodes.summary: Conditionally renders all modal overlay components based on current overlay state, acting as a central overlay switcher.

nodes.name: branding.tsx
nodes.summary: Branding panel components for the TUI sidebar: ASCII art banner, session panel with model/cost/token stats, inline loader animation, and collapse toggle.

nodes.name: SessionPanel
nodes.summary: Large session info panel showing current session metadata, model details, context window usage, cost, and live stats in the sidebar.

nodes.name: fpsOverlay.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal FPS (frames-per-second) debug overlay component that displays current render rate with color coding for performance monitoring.

nodes.name: helpHint.tsx
nodes.summary: Inline help hint component rendering contextual keyboard shortcut hints at the bottom of the TUI based on current mode.

nodes.name: markdown.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured terminal Markdown renderer supporting tables, math blocks, code blocks, inline formatting, autolinks, and media directives, with an LRU-style render cache for performance.

nodes.name: MdImpl
nodes.summary: Core Markdown block renderer handling all block-level elements (headings, lists, code, tables, blockquotes, math, media) with recursive rendering and scroll tracking.

nodes.name: MdInline
nodes.summary: Inline Markdown renderer handling bold, italic, code, links, strikethrough, and other inline formatting elements.

nodes.name: renderTable
nodes.summary: Renders a Markdown table as aligned terminal columns with header divider and cell content.

nodes.name: maskedPrompt.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal password/secret input component that masks typed characters, used for sudo password and secret value overlays.

nodes.name: messageLine.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a single conversation message line in the transcript with role-based styling, tool call indicators, and inline markdown content.

nodes.name: modelPicker.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive model selection overlay allowing users to browse, filter, and switch the active AI model, fetching available models from the backend and persisting the selection.

nodes.name: ModelPicker
nodes.summary: Full model picker overlay component with search filtering, keyboard navigation, provider grouping, and model metadata display.

nodes.name: overlayControls.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared overlay utility hooks and helpers: keyboard binding hook for overlay navigation, hint display, and windowed list utilities for scrollable overlays.

nodes.name: prompts.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive prompt overlay components for tool approval, clarification questions, and confirmation dialogs, each dispatching user responses to the backend gateway.

nodes.name: ApprovalPrompt
nodes.summary: Renders an interactive tool-approval prompt with allow/deny/always options and keyboard shortcuts.

nodes.name: ClarifyPrompt
nodes.summary: Renders a free-text clarification prompt allowing the user to answer agent questions inline before the agent proceeds.

nodes.name: queuedMessages.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays a windowed view of queued/pending user messages waiting to be processed, with helper to compute the visible window of the queue.

nodes.name: sessionPicker.tsx
nodes.summary: Session selection overlay allowing users to resume an existing session from a list, showing session age, title, and last activity.

nodes.name: SessionPicker
nodes.summary: Interactive session picker overlay with keyboard navigation, search, and session metadata display.

nodes.name: skillsHub.tsx
nodes.summary: Skills browser overlay component listing available skills with descriptions and allowing the user to invoke a skill, with search and keyboard navigation.

nodes.name: SkillsHub
nodes.summary: Full skills hub overlay component with list navigation, search filtering, skill invocation, and detail preview.

nodes.name: streamingAssistant.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the live-streaming assistant response area, grouping segments into text blocks and tool call panels and displaying a live todo panel during active turns.

nodes.name: streamingMarkdown.tsx
nodes.summary: Streaming Markdown component that incrementally renders partial assistant output by detecting stable block boundaries and only re-rendering complete blocks.

nodes.name: findStableBoundary
nodes.summary: Determines the largest stable prefix of streaming Markdown text that is safe to render without flickering from incomplete block elements.

nodes.name: textInput.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured terminal text input component for the TUI, implementing grapheme-aware cursor navigation, selection, undo/redo, clipboard operations, voice recording key binding, and optimized fast-path rendering for append and backspace operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Intl.Segmenter for Unicode grapheme cluster navigation; implements a double-buffered fast-path rendering strategy to minimize terminal redraws.

nodes.name: TextInput
nodes.summary: Main React/Ink component that manages the full multi-line text input lifecycle including cursor state, selection, undo stack, paste handling, mouse events, and keyboard dispatch in the terminal TUI.

nodes.name: lineNav
nodes.summary: Computes the next cursor position when navigating up or down across lines in multiline text, using newline offsets to align column position.

nodes.name: graphemeStops
nodes.summary: Returns the array of valid grapheme-cluster boundary byte offsets for a string, using an LRU cache to amortize re-segmentation cost.

nodes.name: shouldPassThroughToGlobalHandler
nodes.summary: Determines whether a key event should bypass the local TextInput handler and propagate to the global application key handler.

nodes.name: themed.tsx
nodes.summary: Exports a minimal `Fg` helper component that renders text in a given foreground color using Ink's Text component, providing a reusable theming primitive for the TUI.

nodes.name: thinking.tsx
nodes.summary: Implements the Thinking panel components for the TUI, including an animated spinner, collapsible tool-call tree with subagent accordions, streaming cursor, heat-colored token display, and the top-level Thinking and ToolTrail exports.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses recursive tree rendering with ASCII box-drawing rail characters for nested subagent call trees.

nodes.name: SubagentAccordion
nodes.summary: Renders a collapsible accordion panel showing subagent activity including its tool calls, streaming output, token usage, and nested child subagents, with expand/collapse toggling.

nodes.name: Spinner
nodes.summary: Animated Ink component that cycles through spinner frames at a configurable interval, exported for use across the TUI.

nodes.name: todoPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the TodoPanel component that displays the agent's active todo list with color-coded row statuses (completed, in-progress, pending) in the terminal TUI.

nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: Exports runtime feature flags derived from environment variables including startup resume ID, startup query, mouse tracking, destructive confirmation bypass, inline mode, and FPS display toggle.

nodes.name: limits.ts
nodes.summary: Defines numeric threshold constants controlling paste size detection, live and history render character/line caps, tail item counts, message length limits, and scroll step sizes for the TUI.

nodes.name: timing.ts
nodes.summary: Exports millisecond timing constants for stream batching in normal, idle, scroll, and typing modes, as well as typing idle detection and reasoning pulse intervals.

nodes.name: charms.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the LONG_RUN_CHARMS constant containing decorative string elements shown during long-running agent operations in the TUI.

nodes.name: faces.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the FACES array of ASCII/emoji face characters used for decorative or status display elements in the TUI.

nodes.name: fortunes.ts
nodes.summary: Provides randomFortune and dailyFortune helpers that select fortune strings from a pool, with the daily variant using a deterministic date-based hash for consistency.

nodes.name: hotkeys.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the HOTKEYS constant array defining keyboard shortcut descriptors displayed in the TUI help or status bar.

nodes.name: placeholders.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a list of PLACEHOLDERS strings and a PLACEHOLDER selector used as hint text in the TUI text input field.

nodes.name: setup.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the SETUP_REQUIRED_TITLE constant and buildSetupRequiredSections helper that constructs UI sections guiding users through the initial API key and configuration setup flow.

nodes.name: verbs.ts
nodes.summary: Exports TOOL_VERBS and VERBS dictionaries mapping tool names and action keys to human-readable verb strings for display in the TUI tool-call trail.

nodes.name: details.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the details panel mode domain logic: parsing and resolving detail section names, cycling through modes, and determining which sections to display based on current state.

nodes.name: messages.ts
nodes.summary: Provides domain utilities for formatting and converting conversation messages: intro message generation, image token metadata, user display strings, transcript conversion, and duration formatting.

nodes.name: paths.ts
nodes.summary: Exports shortCwd and fmtCwdBranch helpers that produce compact current-directory and git-branch display strings for the TUI prompt bar.

nodes.name: providers.ts
nodes.summary: Exports providerDisplayNames, a mapping from provider identifier strings to human-readable display names shown in the TUI.

nodes.name: roles.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the ROLE constant enum-like object defining conversation participant role strings (user, assistant, system) used across the TUI domain layer.

nodes.name: slash.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the TUI_SESSION_MODEL_FLAG constant and two helpers for detecting and parsing slash commands typed into the TUI input.

nodes.name: usage.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a ZERO usage stats object serving as the default/empty token usage value across the TUI domain layer.

nodes.name: viewport.ts
nodes.summary: Implements viewport scroll state logic for the TUI: computing the sticky-prompt position from scroll offset, visible height, and total content height.

nodes.name: entry.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point for the terminal TUI that initialises the Ink renderer, sets up process signal handlers, and mounts the root app component.

nodes.name: gatewayClient.ts
nodes.summary: Implements GatewayClient, the WebSocket/process transport layer that manages spawned or attached hermes-gateway connections, dispatching request/response cycles, sidecar mirroring, log buffering, and ready-state lifecycle.

nodes.name: GatewayClient
nodes.summary: Client class encapsulating the full lifecycle of a hermes-gateway connection — spawning or attaching to the gateway process, managing WebSocket frames, dispatching typed requests, mirroring events to a sidecar, and draining pending calls on shutdown.

nodes.name: gatewayTypes.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all TypeScript types and interfaces for the gateway wire protocol — request/response envelopes, event discriminated unions, streaming message shapes, tool call payloads, and session state structures shared between the TUI and gateway.
nodes.languageNotes: Large discriminated union file with no runtime exports; serves purely as the shared type contract between the TUI client and gateway server.

nodes.name: useCompletion.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that manages tab-completion state for the TUI input, dispatching async RPC requests to the gateway for slash-command and general completions with debounce logic.

nodes.name: completionRequestForInput
nodes.summary: Inspects the current input string to decide whether to request slash-command or general completions, returning a typed completion request descriptor or null.

nodes.name: useCompletion
nodes.summary: Custom hook that tracks completion state, debounces gateway requests, and exposes cycle-through-completions helpers to the input component.

nodes.name: useGitBranch.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that polls the current git branch for a given working directory, caching results in-memory with a TTL and de-duplicating concurrent shell executions.

nodes.name: useGitBranch
nodes.summary: Custom hook that returns the current git branch string, refreshing it on a 5-second interval with stale-while-revalidate caching semantics.

nodes.name: useInputHistory.ts
nodes.summary: Thin React hook wrapper that loads the persistent input history from disk on mount and exposes a ref for navigating through past commands.

nodes.name: useQueue.ts
nodes.summary: React hook managing a FIFO queue of pending agent messages with React-synchronized display state and helpers for enqueue, dequeue, edit, and remove operations.

nodes.name: useQueue
nodes.summary: Custom hook exposing enqueue, dequeue, editAt, and removeAt callbacks backed by a mutable ref queue and a mirrored display state array.

nodes.name: useVirtualHistory.ts
nodes.summary: Complex virtual-scrolling hook for the TUI transcript, computing visible item windows, managing measured heights, sticky live-tail behavior, and overscan clamping across thousands of history items.

nodes.name: useVirtualHistory
nodes.summary: Core virtual-scrolling hook that computes the visible slice of transcript items, tracks individual row heights via Yoga layout measurements, and manages sticky live-tail and overscan clamping.

nodes.name: ensureVirtualItemHeight
nodes.summary: Ensures a virtual item's height entry exists in the heights map, computing it from a provided estimator function if not yet measured.

nodes.name: circularBuffer.ts
nodes.summary: Fixed-capacity circular buffer class for efficiently storing and draining the most recent N items, used for frame-rate and input metrics sampling.

nodes.name: CircularBuffer
nodes.summary: Generic fixed-size ring buffer supporting push, tail-slice, drain, and clear operations with O(1) push and bounded memory.

nodes.name: clipboard.ts
nodes.summary: Cross-platform clipboard read/write helpers that select the appropriate system command (pbpaste/xclip/xsel/clip) per OS and validate clipboard text before use.

nodes.name: readClipboardText
nodes.summary: Attempts to read clipboard text by trying OS-specific commands in order, returning the first valid non-empty string or null.

nodes.name: writeClipboardText
nodes.summary: Writes a text string to the system clipboard by spawning the appropriate OS command and piping the text via stdin.

nodes.name: editor.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the editor resolution utility, verifying that resolveEditor correctly identifies executables from environment variables and PATH.

nodes.name: editor.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves the external text editor to launch by checking VISUAL/EDITOR env vars and falling back to common editors (vim, nano, etc.) found on PATH.

nodes.name: resolveEditor
nodes.summary: Returns the resolved editor command array by checking VISUAL/EDITOR env vars and searching PATH for known fallback editors.

nodes.name: emoji.ts
nodes.summary: Ensures emoji codepoints that default to text presentation have the U+FE0F variation selector appended so terminals render them graphically.

nodes.name: externalCli.ts
nodes.summary: Utility that resolves the Hermes binary path via HERMES_BIN env var and provides a helper to spawn Hermes sub-commands as detached child processes.

nodes.name: forceTruecolor.ts
nodes.summary: Determines whether to force 24-bit truecolor output based on the HERMES_TUI_TRUECOLOR environment variable, returning a boolean for Ink's color configuration.

nodes.name: fpsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store and trackFrame helper that maintains a rolling FPS measurement for the TUI, consuming a circular buffer of frame timestamps.

nodes.name: gracefulExit.ts
nodes.summary: Registers SIGINT/SIGTERM and uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers to run cleanup callbacks before exiting, with a failsafe hard-exit timeout.

nodes.name: setupGracefulExit
nodes.summary: Installs process signal and error handlers that run all registered cleanup functions in parallel before exiting, with a hard-exit failsafe timer.

nodes.name: history.ts
nodes.summary: Manages a file-backed command history store with load and append operations, persisting multi-line entries with a timestamp prefix in a deduplicated format.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the history file, reconstructing multi-line entries from the continuation-line format and returning the most recent N entries.

nodes.name: append
nodes.summary: Appends a new entry to the history file with deduplication, creating the parent directory if needed and encoding multi-line entries with continuation markers.

nodes.name: inputMetrics.ts
nodes.summary: Collection of text-layout computation functions for the TUI composer, calculating visual line breaks, cursor position, column widths, and gutter geometry for multi-line input rendering.

nodes.name: visualLines
nodes.summary: Segments a multi-line string into visual line descriptors, handling Unicode grapheme clusters, terminal column widths, and word-wrap at the specified column count.

nodes.name: cursorLayout
nodes.summary: Returns the row/col cursor position within the visual line grid for a given character offset in the input string.

nodes.name: offsetFromPosition
nodes.summary: Converts a visual (row, col) cursor position back to a character offset in the raw input string, enabling mouse-click-to-cursor mapping.

nodes.name: liveProgress.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the liveProgress message-mutation utilities, verifying appendToolShelfMessage merging, barrier detection, and tool-shelf slot management.

nodes.name: liveProgress.ts
nodes.summary: Provides pure functions for merging in-progress tool-use messages into a transcript message array, enabling collapsible tool-shelf display without message duplication.

nodes.name: appendToolShelfMessage
nodes.summary: Appends or merges a new tool-use message into the previous transcript array, coalescing consecutive tool-shelf messages and respecting barrier boundaries.

nodes.name: mathUnicode.ts
nodes.summary: Large lookup-table and transformation library that converts LaTeX math notation to Unicode characters and symbols for terminal display, including fractions, superscripts, subscripts, and Greek letters.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains ~400 lines of static lookup tables followed by recursive regex-based transformation passes; the main export texToUnicode applies them in a fixed sequence.

nodes.name: texToUnicode
nodes.summary: Transforms a LaTeX math string into a Unicode-rendered equivalent by applying a sequence of regex substitutions for symbols, fractions, super/subscripts, and braced commands.

nodes.name: memory.ts
nodes.summary: Diagnostic utilities for capturing a detailed memory snapshot (heap stats, V8 spaces, active handles, FD count) and optionally writing a heap dump to disk.

nodes.name: captureMemoryDiagnostics
nodes.summary: Collects comprehensive Node.js memory and resource metrics and returns a structured diagnostics object with heap spaces, active handles, open FDs, and inferred leak warnings.

nodes.name: performHeapDump
nodes.summary: Captures a V8 heap snapshot and writes both a JSON diagnostics file and a .heapsnapshot file to a configurable directory.

nodes.name: memoryMonitor.ts
nodes.summary: Background memory monitoring service that polls heap usage on an interval, triggers Ink cache eviction at high watermark, and invokes callbacks at high and critical thresholds with automatic heap dump.

nodes.name: startMemoryMonitor
nodes.summary: Starts an interval-based memory monitor that evicts Ink render caches and dumps the heap when RSS exceeds configurable high or critical thresholds.

nodes.name: messages.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the messages module, verifying appendTranscriptMessage and upsert behavior against transcript arrays.

nodes.name: messages.ts
nodes.summary: Thin transcript mutation helpers that delegate to liveProgress for tool-shelf appending and provide an upsert utility for idempotent role-based message updates.

nodes.name: osc52.ts
nodes.summary: OSC 52 terminal escape sequence helpers for reading and writing clipboard data directly through the terminal emulator, including tmux/screen multiplexer passthrough.

nodes.name: readOsc52Clipboard
nodes.summary: Sends an OSC 52 clipboard query to the terminal and races the response against a timeout, returning the decoded base64 clipboard content or null.

nodes.name: perfPane.tsx
nodes.summary: React Profiler wrapper component and frame-event logging utilities that write per-render timing data to a JSONL log file when HERMES_PERF_LOG is enabled.

nodes.name: PerfPane
nodes.summary: React component that wraps children in a React Profiler when PERF_ENABLED, forwarding render callbacks to the file-based event logger.

nodes.name: platform.ts
nodes.summary: Platform detection and keyboard shortcut utilities for the TUI, covering macOS action modifiers, copy shortcuts, remote shell detection, and voice-record key parsing/formatting.

nodes.name: parseVoiceRecordKey
nodes.summary: Parses a raw user-supplied key-combo string into a structured voice-record key descriptor, validating modifiers and reserved characters.

nodes.name: isVoiceToggleKey
nodes.summary: Determines whether a given key event matches the configured (or default) voice-toggle key binding, handling named keys, modifiers, and platform-specific fallbacks.

nodes.name: isCopyShortcut
nodes.summary: Detects whether a key event represents a copy shortcut, accounting for macOS action modifier and remote-shell environments.

nodes.name: precisionWheel.ts
nodes.summary: Provides precision trackpad/wheel scroll state initialization and per-event step computation with direction and modifier awareness.

nodes.name: computePrecisionWheelStep
nodes.summary: Computes the scroll step magnitude for a precision-wheel (trackpad) event given current state, direction, modifier key, and timestamp.

nodes.name: reasoning.ts
nodes.summary: Splits extended-thinking content from regular response text by parsing reasoning XML tags, and provides a quick tag-presence check.

nodes.name: splitReasoning
nodes.summary: Parses a string containing `<thinking>` and `<thinking>` XML blocks, extracting their content into a separate reasoning string while returning the cleaned response text.

nodes.name: rpc.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight RPC helpers that normalise raw values into typed result and command-dispatch objects, and extract human-readable error messages.

nodes.name: asCommandDispatch
nodes.summary: Coerces a raw RPC payload into a structured CommandDispatch object, branching on result type and surfacing typed fields.

nodes.name: subagentTree.ts
nodes.summary: Builds and traverses a hierarchical subagent execution tree, computing aggregate metrics (tokens, cost, duration), sparkline visualizations, depth-width layout data, and hotness coloring for the TUI display.

nodes.name: buildSubagentTree
nodes.summary: Constructs a tree of SubagentNode objects from a flat list of agent items, grouping children by parent ID and sorting siblings chronologically.

nodes.name: treeTotals
nodes.summary: Recursively accumulates cost, token, and duration totals across all nodes in a subagent tree.

nodes.name: formatSummary
nodes.summary: Formats accumulated subagent tree totals (agents, duration, tokens, cost) into a human-readable summary string for the TUI header.

nodes.name: sparkline
nodes.summary: Renders an array of numeric values as a Unicode block-character sparkline string for in-terminal activity visualization.

nodes.name: syntax.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight inline syntax highlighter for code blocks in the TUI, supporting a fixed set of languages via regex-based token matching without a full parser.

nodes.name: highlightLine
nodes.summary: Tokenizes a single source-code line into styled segments (comments, strings, keywords, identifiers) based on the resolved language spec.

nodes.name: terminalModes.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the ANSI escape sequence for resetting terminal modes and exposes a helper to write it synchronously or asynchronously to a stream.

nodes.name: terminalParity.ts
nodes.summary: Detects macOS terminal context (iTerm2, VS Code, remote SSH) and generates user-facing hints about required terminal configuration for full TUI feature parity.

nodes.name: terminalParityHints
nodes.summary: Produces a list of onboarding hint strings describing which terminal setup steps are needed for a user's detected environment.

nodes.name: terminalSetup.ts
nodes.summary: Automates detection of VS Code-like terminals and programmatic injection of required keybindings into terminal config files, including conflict detection and backup logic.

nodes.name: configureTerminalKeybindings
nodes.summary: Reads a VS Code-style keybindings JSON file, detects conflicts with required TUI bindings, and writes updated keybindings with backups.

nodes.name: configureDetectedTerminalKeybindings
nodes.summary: Detects the current terminal emulator and delegates to configureTerminalKeybindings for automatic setup.

nodes.name: shouldPromptForTerminalSetup
nodes.summary: Determines whether the TUI should show a terminal setup prompt by checking if required keybindings are already present in the terminal's config.

nodes.name: stripJsonComments
nodes.summary: Removes single-line and block comments from a JSON string to allow parsing VS Code-style JSONC keybindings files.

nodes.name: text.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for text utility functions from text.ts, covering edge cases in text processing and formatting.

nodes.name: text.ts
nodes.summary: Core TUI text utilities including ANSI stripping, markdown-to-plaintext rendering, bounded text truncation, tool-trail line building/parsing, row estimation, and thinking-text cleanup.

nodes.name: boundedRenderText
nodes.summary: Truncates text to a character and line budget, prepending a label prefix and adding an ellipsis summary when the text is clipped.

nodes.name: estimateRows
nodes.summary: Estimates the number of terminal rows required to render a text block at a given width, accounting for code fences, markdown tables, and word-wrap.

nodes.name: buildToolTrailLine
nodes.summary: Assembles a formatted tool-trail log line with tool name, context preview, optional error/note, and duration.

nodes.name: parseToolTrailResultLine
nodes.summary: Parses a serialized tool-trail result line back into its structured components (tool name, context, error, note, duration).

nodes.name: renderEstimateLine
nodes.summary: Converts a single markdown line to its plain-text TUI rendering, handling tables, images, links, emphasis, and list markers.

nodes.name: todo.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for todo glyph and tone helper functions from todo.ts.

nodes.name: todo.ts
nodes.summary: Provides tiny helpers that map todo item status values to display glyphs and color tones for the TUI.

nodes.name: viewportStore.ts
nodes.summary: Exposes React hooks (useViewportSnapshot, useScrollbarSnapshot) that subscribe to a scroll store and derive stable viewport and scrollbar state snapshots for rendering.

nodes.name: useViewportSnapshot
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to a scroll store via useSyncExternalStore and returns a memoized viewport snapshot (scrollTop, height, contentHeight, sticky flag).

nodes.name: useScrollbarSnapshot
nodes.summary: React hook that derives scrollbar geometry (track height, thumb height, thumb offset) from the scroll store for rendering a custom scrollbar.

nodes.name: virtualHeights.ts
nodes.summary: Computes cache keys and estimated terminal-row heights for conversation messages to support virtual scrolling in the TUI.

nodes.name: estimatedMsgHeight
nodes.summary: Estimates the number of terminal rows a conversation message will occupy based on its content, role, compact mode, and terminal column width.

nodes.name: wheelAccel.ts
nodes.summary: Implements mouse-wheel scroll acceleration for both native terminal and xterm.js hosts, with configurable base speed and separate step algorithms for each mode.

nodes.name: nativeStep
nodes.summary: Computes the scroll step for native terminal mode, applying exponential acceleration based on inter-event timing and velocity history.

nodes.name: xtermJsStep
nodes.summary: Computes the scroll step for xterm.js host mode, scaling by pixel delta and applying exponential acceleration with a separate velocity model.

nodes.name: interpolation.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a regex constant and helper for detecting template interpolation syntax (`{!...}`) in protocol strings.

nodes.name: paste.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the single regex constant used to detect paste-snippet markers in protocol message text.

nodes.name: theme.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the TUI color theme system including dark/light preset themes, ANSI color math (luminance, HSL, 256-color mapping), light-mode detection from environment variables, and a factory function for creating custom skins.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements WCAG-style luminance calculations and xterm 256-color palette math to ensure readable ANSI colors across terminal themes.

nodes.name: fromSkin
nodes.summary: Constructs a full Theme object from a user-supplied color palette and branding options, applying light-mode normalization as needed.

nodes.name: detectLightMode
nodes.summary: Detects whether the terminal is running in light mode by inspecting environment variables (HERMES_TUI_LIGHT, HERMES_TUI_THEME, COLORFGBG, TERM_PROGRAM) in priority order.

nodes.name: bestReadableAnsiColor
nodes.summary: Selects the nearest ANSI 256-color palette entry to an RGB input that maximizes readability against the terminal background using luminance contrast and hue distance scoring.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type and interface definitions for the TUI layer, covering messages, tool calls, subagent items, viewport state, and theme structures.

nodes.name: hermes-ink.d.ts
nodes.summary: Ambient TypeScript declaration file for the hermes-ink rendering library, typing its React component props and exported APIs for use throughout the TUI.

nodes.name: tsconfig.build.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for production builds of the TUI package, extending the base tsconfig with output directory overrides.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Base TypeScript compiler configuration for the ui-tui package, defining compiler options, includes, and excludes for the TUI source tree.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test runner configuration for the ui-tui package, setting up the test environment for TypeScript unit tests.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Hermes Agent web UI covering the tech stack (React, Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui), dev and build commands, and the src/ directory structure.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat-config for the web package applying TypeScript-aware and React-hooks linting rules.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite entry HTML shell that mounts the React app at the #root div and loads the TypeScript entry point.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the web UI defining React 18, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and i18n dependencies alongside dev, build, lint, and preview scripts.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that assembles plugin-aware routing, sidebar navigation partitioning, and the responsive shell layout for the Hermes Agent web UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useMemo extensively to derive nav items and route tables from plugin manifests at runtime.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Main exported component that renders the full application shell including responsive sidebar, plugin routes, and theme/toast/i18n providers.

nodes.name: buildNavItems
nodes.summary: Builds a sorted list of sidebar navigation items by merging built-in routes with plugin manifest entries using position hints.

nodes.name: buildRoutes
nodes.summary: Constructs the React Router route configuration by merging built-in routes with plugin-provided overrides and addon routes.

nodes.name: SidebarSystemActions
nodes.summary: Renders the system-action buttons in the sidebar (settings, about, etc.) using the useSystemActions hook and handles navigation on click.

nodes.name: AutoField.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic form field component that renders the correct input type (checkbox, select, number, textarea, array, object, text) based on a JSON-schema field definition.

nodes.name: AutoField
nodes.summary: Exported component that auto-selects the appropriate HTML input element for a schema key and calls onChange with the properly-typed value.

nodes.name: Backdrop.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated canvas backdrop component that renders a GPU-tier-aware gradient animation as the application background.

nodes.name: ChatSidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Chat-specific sidebar panel that connects to the agent gateway via WebSocket, creates a session, streams tool-call events, and renders a live tool-call list with a model picker.

nodes.languageNotes: Custom hermesDevToken Vite plugin scrapes the Python server's index.html on each dev page load to inject window.__HERMES_SESSION_TOKEN__ and window.__HERMES_DASHBOARD_EMBEDDED_CHAT__, solving a session token bootstrapping problem specific to the Vite dev server.
